cot_prefix: Okay, let me try to figure out the answer here. The question is asking how the author introduces the topic. The passage starts with a series of questions like "Do you start your work by making a list..." and others. Then it goes on to list reasons why making a to-do list is good. The options are A through D. So, the first part of the text is definitely asking questions to the reader. The author is using those questions to engage the reader and lead into discussing the benefits of to-do lists. The next paragraph starts with "Here are some reasons," which is followed by the listed benefits. But the question is about how the topic is introduced, not the entire content. Looking at the options: A says "asking a series of questions," which matches the opening. B is presenting facts, but the opening isn't just facts; it's questions.
